title: No-fixed transition-preserving alignment
summary: The current published taxonomy uses the no-fixed repair-loopback full-cases grouping. Sentences are not removed before state construction, pairwise alignment, or group optimization. LLM-judged no-information rows are projected to reserved group -1 so transitions into and out of degenerate text remain visible without treating the text as a semantic taxonomy category.
note: Hard sentence removal remains rejected. The published explorer uses generated no-fixed group labels and all-case annotations with a reserved degenerate projection state.

rows.label: No-fixed canonical
rows.note: Repair-loopback full-cases grouping without fixed U1/U7 anchors. All sentences are preserved for alignment and grouping.

rows.label: Reserved degenerate projection
rows.note: Rows judged no-information are assigned to group -1 during annotation. Only transitions to and from that state are interpreted.

rows.label: Hard sentence removal
rows.note: Removing no-information sentences before state and pairwise construction degraded the grouping score, so this is not production.
